edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SmolLM3-3B chat model is evaluated on BFCL via a fork of huggingface/gorilla (berkeley-function-call-leaderboard) with a dedicated SmolLM3-3B parser, since lighteval does not yet support BFCL.

evidence.excerpt: BFCL is not yet supported by LightEval, so we used a fork ... with a dedicated parser for SmolLM3-3B.
evidence.explanation: README documents BFCL evaluation of SmolLM3-3B via a forked Gorilla berkeley-function-call-leaderboard repo.
Both eval tables report SmolLM3-3B BFCL tool-calling scores.
Row reports SmolLM3-3B's BFCL score under no-thinking; a parallel row reports the extended-thinking score.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SmolLM3's chat template borrows Qwen3's pattern of pre-filling the model's response with empty think blocks in non-reasoning mode to ensure direct answers.

evidence.excerpt: In non-reasoning mode, we pre-fill the model's response with empty think blocks, similar to Qwen3, to ensure direct answers without explicit reasoning.
evidence.explanation: Cites Qwen3's empty-think-block pattern as the inspiration for SmolLM3's non-reasoning-mode chat template.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SmolLM3-3B (chat / dual instruct+reasoning) is initialized from SmolLM3-3B-Base after long-context extension and then post-trained via reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and merging.

evidence.excerpt: Base model: https://hf.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base Instruct and reasoning model: https://hf.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
evidence.explanation: Both releases share the same SmolLM3-3B family; the chat release continues training from the Base checkpoint via the documented post-training pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: base_model: - HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base ... Post-training included midtraining on 140B reasoning tokens followed by supervised fine-tuning and alignment via Anchored Preference Optimization (APO).
evidence.explanation: Frontmatter declares the weight inheritance and the summary describes the post-training continuation from Base to chat.

evidence.excerpt:
base_model:
  - HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base
evidence.explanation: HuggingFace YAML metadata declares SmolLM3-3B-Base as the parent checkpoint for SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.excerpt: The pretraining consists of these stages ... After the main pretraining we improved the model in a mid-training stage for long context and reasoning ... The resulting model is the checkpoint we are releasing today.
evidence.explanation: Source describes the continuous training cascade from the Base model through mid-training, post-training, and merging into the final released SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.excerpt:
model_name_or_path: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base
model_revision: main
evidence.explanation: Pins the 3B-Base revision as the mid-training start.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Reasoning mid-training (35B tokens, 4 epochs ~140B) sources general reasoning data from open-thoughts/OpenThoughts3-1.2M.

evidence.excerpt: Our mid-training dataset contained 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought's OpenThoughts3-1.2M
evidence.explanation: Names OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a reasoning-mid-training source.
Identifies OpenThoughts3-1.2M as the second component of the smoltalk2 Mid mix with row/token weight.

evidence.excerpt: OpenThoughts3-1.2M: 1133524 rows. ... OpenThoughts3-1.2M: 435193 rows where we remove the reasoning trace.
evidence.explanation: Cites OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a SFT mix component in both think and no_think forms.

evidence.excerpt: Our mid-training dataset contained 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought's OpenThoughts3-1.2M
evidence.explanation: Source explicitly names OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a reasoning-mid-training data source.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Reasoning mid-training also sources data from a subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 containing R1-generated reasoning traces.

evidence.excerpt: and a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: Names NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset v1.1 as the second reasoning-mid-training source.

evidence.excerpt: Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset: 3.64M rows ... Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset_reasoning_r1 | 1 | 3644790 | 76.25 | 18707.9 | 53.19
evidence.explanation: Identifies the Llama-Nemotron reasoning_r1 split as half of the Mid mix and gives row/token weight.

evidence.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: Source names the Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset (v1.1) as the second reasoning-mid-training source.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The Llama-Nemotron subset used in mid-training carries reasoning traces generated by DeepSeek-R1; SmolLM3-3B's reasoning data therefore originates from R1 outputs.

evidence.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: States that the mid-training data includes R1-generated reasoning traces, making DeepSeek-R1 a generator of training content for SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: Source attributes the reasoning traces in the dataset to DeepSeek R1, making R1 the upstream generator of mid-training content.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The mid-training mixture is also released as part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'mid' subset), per author confirmations in the blog comments.

evidence.excerpt: We released them in SmolTalk2. The dataset has the Mid, and SFT and Preference subsets.
evidence.explanation: Author confirms that the mid-training data is released within HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2.

evidence.excerpt: We released all the datasets we used for training in SmolTalk2. The dataset card details which datasets were generated using Qwen3-32B.
evidence.explanation: Author confirms SmolTalk2 is the release of all training datasets, and the dataset card identifies Qwen3-32B-generated subsets.

evidence.excerpt: We released them in SmolTalk2. The dataset has the Mid, and SFT and Preference subsets.
evidence.explanation: Author confirms that the APO preference mixture is released in HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2.

evidence.excerpt: three subsets (Mid, SFT, Preference) that correspond to the three phases of Post-Training for SmolLM3-3B ... The mid-training dataset has a total of 4.8M rows
evidence.explanation: Designates the Mid config of smoltalk2 as the mid-training data for SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.excerpt: The total mix consists of 25 datasets, which we decontaminated to remove samples present in the benchmarks used for evaluation
evidence.explanation: Identifies the SFT config of smoltalk2 as SmolLM3-3B's SFT data.

evidence.excerpt: We used two datasets to train SmolLM3-3B with APO, which has a total of 447k rows.
evidence.explanation: Designates the Preference config of smoltalk2 as APO data for SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.excerpt:
id: HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2
      config: Mid
      split: Llama_Nemotron_Post_Training_Dataset_reasoning_r1 ... split: OpenThoughts3_1.2M
evidence.explanation: Two splits of smoltalk2 'Mid' config drive mid-training.

evidence.excerpt: id: HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 ... config: SFT ... split: smoltalk_smollm3_everyday_conversations_no_think ... split: tulu_3_sft_personas_instruction_following_no_think ... split: hermes_function_calling_v1_no_think ... split: OpenHermes_2.5_no_think ... split: OpenThoughts3_1.2M_no_think_no_think ... split: smolagents_toolcalling_traces_think ... split: s1k_1.1_think
evidence.explanation: YAML enumerates the 25 smoltalk2 SFT splits used.

evidence.excerpt:
config: Preference
      split: llama_3.1_tulu_3_8b_preference_mixture_no_think ... split: tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think
evidence.explanation: Two preference splits drive APO-zero training.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B in reasoning (thinking) mode generated synthetic reasoning traces used in SFT for domains lacking such data (multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, everyday conversations); these synthetic completions form a portion of the 10 reasoning-mode SFT datasets.

evidence.excerpt: we generated synthetic data by prompting Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B (thinking mode) as the generator of synthetic reasoning training data for SFT.

evidence.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as 'chosen' and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as 'rejected' for alignment with Anchored Preference Optimization.
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B as the chosen-completion generator for SmolLM3 APO preference pairs.

evidence.excerpt: SmolTalk (multilingual-8languages): 244736 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in SmolTalk. ... generate responses with Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode.
evidence.explanation: Multiple think splits explicitly cite Qwen3-32B as their response generator.

evidence.excerpt: Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (`think`): 216k rows where we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B as the chosen-completion generator for the think preference split.

evidence.excerpt: we generated synthetic data by prompting Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets
evidence.explanation: Source identifies Qwen3-32B as the generator of synthetic reasoning-mode SFT data.

evidence.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as "chosen" and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as "rejected" for alignment with Anchored Preference Optimization.
evidence.explanation: Source identifies Qwen3-32B as the chosen-completion generator for APO.

evidence.excerpt: split: smoltalk_everyday_convs_reasoning_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: smoltalk_systemchats_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: LongAlign_64k_Qwen3_32B_yarn_131k_think ... split: aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: smoltalk_multilingual8_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: table_gpt_Qwen3_32B_think
evidence.explanation: Six SFT splits explicitly name Qwen3-32B (thinking) as the trace generator.

evidence.excerpt: split: tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think
evidence.explanation: Split name encodes Qwen3-32B as the larger generator (chosen).

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: APO non-reasoning preference data uses the Tulu 3 preference mixture (allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-8b-preference-mixture).

evidence.excerpt: a combination of the Tulu3 preference dataset for non-reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: Names the Tulu3 preference dataset as the non-reasoning APO source; the released form is allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-8b-preference-mixture.

evidence.excerpt: [Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (`no_think`)](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-8b-preference-mixture): 231k rows. Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (`think`): 216k rows where we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B and the rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B.
evidence.explanation: Cites the Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture as the no_think component and prompt source for the think variant.

evidence.excerpt: we performed a round of model alignment using a combination of the Tulu3 preference dataset for non-reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: Source names the Tulu3 preference dataset (linked to allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-8b-preference-mixture in the blog post) as the non-reasoning preference data.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Qwen3-0.6B generated the rejected completions for SmolLM3's reasoning-mode synthetic preference pairs used in APO.

evidence.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as 'chosen' and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as 'rejected' for alignment with Anchored Preference Optimization.
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-0.6B as the rejected-completion generator for SmolLM3 APO preference pairs.

evidence.excerpt: and the rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B.
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-0.6B as the rejected-completion generator for the think preference split.

evidence.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as "chosen" and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as "rejected" for alignment with Anchored Preference Optimization.
evidence.explanation: Source identifies Qwen3-0.6B as the rejected-completion generator for APO.

evidence.excerpt: split: tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think
evidence.explanation: Split name encodes Qwen3-0.6B as the smaller generator (rejected).

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The APO alignment recipe (off-policy preference optimization with a published preference mixture) follows the Tulu 3 alignment lineage; SmolLM3 directly uses the Tulu 3 preference dataset for the non-reasoning portion.

evidence.excerpt: a combination of the Tulu3 preference dataset for non-reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: Reuse of the Tulu3 preference dataset signals methodological alignment with Tulu 3's off-policy preference optimization recipe.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT mix sources prompts and conversations from the original SmolTalk (v1) for the multilingual-8languages, everyday-conversations, systemchats-30k, smollm3_smol-magpie-ultra, smollm3_explore-instruct-rewriting, smollm3_smol-rewrite, smollm3_smol-summarize splits (both think and no_think variants).

evidence.excerpt: SmolTalk (multilingual-8languages): 244736 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in SmolTalk. ... SmolTalk (everyday-conversations) ... SmolTalk (systemchats-30k) ...
evidence.explanation: Repeated explicit citations of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk as the prompt source for many SFT splits.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed open-r1/s1K-1.1 (835 rows) as the s1k-1.1_think split.

evidence.excerpt: [s1k-1.1](https://huggingface.co/datasets/open-r1/s1K-1.1): 835 rows.
evidence.explanation: Cites s1K-1.1 as a SFT think component.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed the Aya dataset (15222 rows) with responses generated by Qwen3-32B against Aya prompts.

evidence.excerpt: [Aya](https://huggingface.co/datasets/CohereLabs/aya_dataset): 15222 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in Aya.
evidence.explanation: Cites Aya as the prompt source for the aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think split.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed Table-GPT in both forms: 13201 rows with responses regenerated by Qwen3-32B (think) and 13203 rows of the original (no_think).

evidence.excerpt: [Table-GPT](https://huggingface.co/datasets/LipengCS/Table-GPT): 13201 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in Table-GPT. ... [Table-GPT](https://huggingface.co/datasets/LipengCS/Table-GPT): 13203 rows.
evidence.explanation: Cites LipengCS/Table-GPT as a SFT mix component in both think and no_think forms.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed the science split of Mixture-of-Thoughts (86110 rows, no_think variant where the reasoning trace was removed).

evidence.excerpt: [Mixture of Thoughts](https://huggingface.co/datasets/open-r1/Mixture-of-Thoughts) (science): 86110 rows where we remove the reasoning trace.
evidence.explanation: Cites Mixture-of-Thoughts science split as a SFT no_think component.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed Tulu 3 SFT Personas IF in two roles: directly as 29970 no_think rows, and as the prompt source for the multi-turn-reasoning-if_think split (28217 rows).

evidence.excerpt: [Tulu 3 SFT Personas IF](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following): 29970 rows. ... We source prompts from [Tulu 3 Personas IF](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following), generate 2 verifiable turns using Qwen3-235B-A22B
evidence.explanation: Cites Tulu 3 Personas IF as both a SFT no_think component and the prompt source of the Multi-Turn IF think split.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed hermes-function-calling-v1 (8961 rows, no_think) for tool/function calling capability.

evidence.excerpt: [hermes-function-calling-v1](https://huggingface.co/datasets/NousResearch/hermes-function-calling-v1): 8961 rows.
evidence.explanation: Cites hermes-function-calling-v1 as a SFT no_think component.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed OpenHermes-2.5 (384900 rows, no_think) at weight 0.5.

evidence.excerpt: [OpenHermes-2.5](https://huggingface.co/datasets/teknium/OpenHermes-2.5): 384900 rows.
evidence.explanation: Cites teknium/OpenHermes-2.5 as a SFT no_think component.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SFT consumed LongAlign as the prompt source for the LongAlign-64k splits: think variant (7526 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with yarn-131k context, e.g. LongAlign-64k-Qwen3-32B-yarn-131k_think) and no_think variant (LongAlign-64k-context-lang-annotated lang_6, 6249 rows filtered to <=64k tokens).

evidence.excerpt: LongAlign-64k-context-lang-annotated: 7526 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in LongAlign-64k. ... We filter [LongAlign](https://huggingface.co/datasets/THUDM/LongAlign-10k) for samples up to 64k tokens.
evidence.explanation: Cites LongAlign-10k (THUDM/LongAlign-10k, now zai-org) as the prompt source for both LongAlign SFT splits.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The multi-turn-reasoning-if_think SFT split follows the methodology of Meta's Multi-IF (https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15553) for synthesizing multi-turn instruction-following data, adopting its schema of 2 follow-up verifiable turns per prompt.

evidence.excerpt: We follow [Multi-IF's approach](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15553) to generate multi turn data.
evidence.explanation: Explicit methodology borrowing from Multi-IF for the Multi-Turn IF data construction.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The smoltalk-multilingual-8languages no_think split follows the Qwen 2.5 report's translation recipe: translate Smol-Magpie-Ultra and Smol-Constraints prompts into 8 target languages (fr, es, it, pt, de, ar, ru, zh) respecting local conventions, then generate target-language answers.

evidence.excerpt: Following [Qwen 2.5 report](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2412.15115), we first translate the prompts in Smol-Magpie-Ultra and Smol-Constraints using Qwen to 8 languages (fr, es, it, pt, de, ar, ru, zh) while respecting local conventions
evidence.explanation: Explicit methodology borrowing from the Qwen 2.5 multilingual translation pipeline.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: RULER used to evaluate SmolLM3 long-context performance — including detecting the degradation introduced by reasoning mid-training and confirming recovery up to 128k after the final model merge.

evidence.excerpt: we observed performance degradation on long context benchmarks like RULER ... We were able to recover the base model's RULER score on contexts up to 128k tokens.
evidence.explanation: RULER repeatedly named as the long-context benchmark for SmolLM3.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: HELMET used as a long-context evaluation benchmark during the long-context-extension ablations of SmolLM3 mid-training.

evidence.excerpt: didn't further boost performance on RULER and HELMET benchmarks
evidence.explanation: HELMET named alongside RULER as a long-context benchmark for the long-context ablation.
